varieties.name: Vincent
varieties.origin: China, developed for subtropical climates
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit, egg-shaped, fuzzy brown skin, vigorous vine growth, excellent low-chill variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright green flesh, sweet tropical flavor, medium size (80-100g), excellent eating quality
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, produces large crops consistently
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates - Only 100-200 chill hours required. Thrives in warm subtropical climates. Tolerates high humidity. Pairs with Tomuri male pollinator. Well-suited to Humid subtropical mild winters and warm summers. Disease resistant.
varieties.availability: Available from Queensland and NSW nurseries, Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery

varieties.name: Sweetie (Daleys Selection)
varieties.origin: Australia, selected by Daleys Nursery from golden kiwi seedling, 2000s
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit, vigorous hardy growth, specifically bred for subtropical Queensland
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow-green flesh, sweet flavor, smooth texture
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates - Very low chill requirement specifically selected for subtropics. Perfect for humid subtropical climates gardens. Australian-bred for local conditions. Requires specific male pollinator also from Daleys.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Kyogle NSW

varieties.name: Hayward Seedling (Queensland Selection)
varieties.origin: Queensland, Australia, selected as Hayward seedling adapted to warmer climates, 1990s
varieties.traits: Similar to Hayward but slightly smaller, more elongated shape, adapted to warmer winters
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light-green flesh, good flavor, typical fuzzy brown skin
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates - Relatively low chill requirement compared to standard Hayward. Originated in Queensland, well-adapted to humid subtropical climates climate. Handles humid subtropical conditions better than traditional Hayward.

varieties.name: Elmwood
varieties.origin: New Zealand, developed for low-chill regions
varieties.traits: Extremely vigorous growth, large showy fragrant blossoms, very large fruit (up to 280g/10 oz)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Green flesh, excellent flavor, extremely large kiwi-shaped fruits
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields, bears fruit at young age (200-260 lbs per mature vine)

varieties.name: Abbott
varieties.origin: New Zealand, early selection from Chinese germplasm, 1920s-1930s
varieties.traits: Early flowering and maturing, medium-sized round fruit, densely hairy brown skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light-green flesh, very sweet taste, lower ascorbic acid, medium titratable acidity

varieties.name: Dexter
varieties.origin: Australia/California, developed by Dr. David Dexter, 1970s
varieties.traits: Medium size, slightly elongated, quite hairy, compact growth habit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Green flesh, good flavor, sweet tropical taste

varieties.name: Blake
varieties.origin: New Zealand, HortResearch breeding program, 1990s
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit, teardrop-shaped, early maturing (6-8 weeks before Hayward)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden-brown smooth skin, juicy sweet flesh, slightly more acidic than Hayward
varieties.yield_notes: Precocious variety, bears fruit one year earlier than other varieties, heavy yields
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR humid subtropical climates - Low-chill requirement. Very early ripening perfect for areas with early autumn frosts. Partially self-fruitful. Vigorous disease-resistant vines. Good storage capacity. Flowers very early. Excellent for extending harvest season.

varieties.name: Allison
varieties.origin: New Zealand, chance seedling discovered 1920s, named after Alexander Allison
varieties.traits: Medium-sized oblong fruit, densely hairy brownish skin, early maturing
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light-green flesh, good flavor, good balance of sweetness and acidity

varieties.name: Bruno
varieties.origin: New Zealand, named after Bruno Just, early nurseryman, 1930s-1940s
varieties.traits: Large elongated cylindrical fruit, dark brown skin with short dense hairs
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light-green flesh, good flavor, higher ascorbic acid content than Abbott

varieties.name: Saanichton 12
varieties.origin: Vancouver Island, British Columbia, Canada, developed for coastal climate, 1980s
varieties.traits: Medium-large fruit, vigorous growth, cold-hardy in mild climates
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet good-tasting fruit, typical green flesh

varieties.name: Issai
varieties.origin: Japan, 1980s
varieties.traits: Small grape-sized fruit, smooth hairless skin, less vigorous than other kiwiberries (12-20 feet)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Green flesh, very sweet flavor similar to regular kiwi, kumquat-sized
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR humid subtropical climates - Self-fertile (hermaphrodite flowers) - no male needed! Prefers filtered light in SE Queensland. Grows well under 30% shade cloth. Less vigorous makes it perfect for smaller humid subtropical climates gardens or containers. Hardy to Zone 5 but tolerates subtropical heat.
varieties.availability: Fruit Tree Cottage (SE Queensland), Australian nurseries

varieties.name: Tomuri (Male)
varieties.origin: New Zealand
varieties.traits: Late-blooming male, cream-colored flowers in June, strong vigorous grower
varieties.yield_notes: One male can pollinate up to 8 female vines
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR humid subtropical climates - Very low chill requirement (100 hours). Perfect match for Vincent. Extended bloom duration covers late-flowering females. Essential for subtropical low-chill plantings. Requires summer pruning due to vigor.

varieties.name: Matua (Male)
varieties.origin: New Zealand
varieties.traits: Early to mid-season blooming, large heart-shaped fuzzy foliage, burgundy shoots, vigorous
varieties.yield_notes: Can pollinate up to 8 female vines, long blooming period
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR humid subtropical climates - Low chill requirement (200 hours). Very versatile pollinator with extended bloom time. Also adds ornamental value with attractive foliage. Hardy to Zone 7. Excellent for Saanichton pairing.

varieties.name: Hayward
varieties.origin: New Zealand, named after Hayward Wright, 1920s-1930s, introduced commercially 1940s
varieties.traits: Large oval fruit (120-150g), fuzzy brown skin, world standard variety, 90% of commercial production
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright emerald-green flesh, excellent sweet-tangy flavor, high vitamin C, superior quality
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, reliable high yields once established

varieties.name: Hayward
varieties.origin: New Zealand, 1920s-1930s
varieties.traits: Large oval fruit, fuzzy brown skin, world's most cultivated variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright green flesh, excellent sweet-tangy flavor, high vitamin C

varieties.name: Allison
varieties.origin: New Zealand, 1920s
varieties.traits: Medium oblong fruit, densely hairy skin, early maturing
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light-green flesh, good balanced flavor

varieties.name: Abbott
varieties.origin: New Zealand, 1920s-1930s
varieties.traits: Early flowering, medium round fruit, densely hairy
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, lower acid content

varieties.name: Bruno
varieties.origin: New Zealand, 1930s-1940s
varieties.traits: Large elongated fruit, dark brown dense hairs
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light-green flesh, good flavor, high vitamin C

varieties.name: Monty (Montgomery)
varieties.origin: New Zealand, chance seedling discovered early 1950s, introduced 1957
varieties.traits: Small to medium oblong fruit, somewhat angular, widest at apex, dense hairs
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light-green flesh, good flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Highly vigorous and productive, sometimes excessively so, late-ripening

varieties.name: Tomuri (Male)
varieties.origin: New Zealand
varieties.traits: Late-blooming male, vigorous growth
varieties.growing_notes: RECOMMENDED FOR tropical monsoon climates - Most commonly used male pollinator in India. Widely recommended by Indian agricultural authorities. Extended bloom covers late-flowering females. Essential for commercial orchards. Vigorous growth needs management in Tropical monsoon fertile conditions.

varieties.name: Matua (Male)
varieties.origin: New Zealand
varieties.traits: Early to mid-season blooming, vigorous
varieties.yield_notes: One male pollinates up to 8 females

varieties.name: Hort16A (Zespri Gold)
varieties.origin: New Zealand, HortResearch breeding program, developed 1991, launched 1997-2000
varieties.traits: Smooth bronze skin with short silky hair, medium to large fruit, pointed tip
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright yellow flesh, tropical sweet flavor (notes of pineapple/mango), less acidic than green
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, blooms 4 weeks ahead of Hayward

varieties.name: Jintao (Jin Gold)
varieties.origin: China, selected 1981 by Wuhan Institute of Botany from wild plants in Jiangxi Province, commercial 2001
varieties.traits: Golden-yellow smooth skin, medium fruit, highly productive, robust
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow flesh, exceptional sweetness, high sugar content, aromatic
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, earlier than Hayward, disease-resistant
varieties.growing_notes: SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates HILLS - Chinese origin may suit subcontinental conditions better. More robust and disease-resistant than Hort16A. Widely grown in China and Italy. Similar quality to Zespri Gold. High essential amino acids. Adapts to various climatic conditions. Good shelf life.
varieties.availability: May be available through Indian agricultural research institutes

varieties.name: Hongyang (Red Heart Kiwi)
varieties.origin: China, Sichuan Province, seeds collected 1982, officially registered 1997
varieties.traits: Cylindrical fruit flattened at ends, smooth brown slightly hairy skin, medium size (90g average, up to 150g)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Red-purple flesh around core radiating outward, yellow-green outer flesh, striking decorative appearance, sweet aromatic tropical taste, few or no seeds
varieties.yield_notes: Precocious, high field production, most widely planted variety in China
